The **29th Fantasia International Film Festival** opened on July 16 in [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada, with American neo-Western black comedy film *[Eddington](/wiki/Eddington_(film) "Eddington (film)")*, by [Ari Aster](/wiki/Ari_Aster "Ari Aster").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The Canadian Trailblazer Award was presented to Hungarian-born Canadian filmmaker [George Mihalka](/wiki/George_Mihalka "George Mihalka"), the director of the 1981 slasher film *[My Bloody Valentine](/wiki/My_Bloody_Valentine_(film) "My Bloody Valentine (film)")*, and [Sheila McCarthy](/wiki/Sheila_McCarthy "Sheila McCarthy"), Canadian actress and singer during festival.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[Pascal Plante](/wiki/Pascal_Plante "Pascal Plante"), a Canadian film director and screenwriter served as jury president for Compétition Cheval Noir. The seven of the competition’s eight categories awards were announced on July 26, with the jury awarding *Mother of Flies*, a horror–fantasy film by John Adams, Zelda Adams, and Toby Posner, with the Cheval Noir Award for Best Film, marking the first time a United States production has received this award.[[4]](#cite_note-award:variety-4)

The festival closed on August 3, 2025 with dog sex adult comedy *[Fixed](/wiki/Fixed_(film) "Fixed (film)")*, by Russian-American animator, producer–director, [Genndy Tartakovsky](/wiki/Genndy_Tartakovsky "Genndy Tartakovsky"), who was also honored with 'Cheval Noir Career Achievement Award' along with [Danny Elfman](/wiki/Danny_Elfman "Danny Elfman"), American film composer, and musician.[[5]](#cite_note-var:full-5) In the Audience Awards announced on August 5, 2025, Best International Feature Gold was awarded to 2025 French film *Flush*, a thriller comedy by Grégory Morin.[[6]](#cite_note-aud:awards-6)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=29th_Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Awards")]

The festival announced its juried awards, with *Mother of Flies*, by John Adams, Zelda Adams, and Toby Poser—collectively known as the Adams Family—receiving the Cheval Noir Award for Best Film. It also received The Sandro Forte Award for Best Motion Picture Score.[[4]](#cite_note-award:variety-4)[[13]](#cite_note-award:iw-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Cheval Noir competition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=29th_Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Cheval Noir competition")]

* Best Film: *Mother of Flies* by John Adams, Zelda Adams and Toby Poser
* Special Jury Mention (Best Film): *New Group* by Yuta Shimotsu
* Best Director: Hadrah Daeng Ratu for *The Book of Sijjin And Illiyyin*
* Best Screenplay: Connor Diedrich and Samuel Johnson for *Terrestrial*, by Steve Pink
